title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat, as it exploits compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code. This can lead to widespread data encryption, service disruption, and potential data exfiltration, impacting business continuity and customer trust.
recommended_actions: Immediately audit and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strong access controls, such as multi-factor authentication (MFA) and least privilege principles.
Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor for anomalous behavior in cloud environments.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored offline or in a secure, isolated environment to protect against ransomware encryption.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: The Keycloak authentication manager in `apache-airflow-providers-keycloak` did not generate or...
description: The Keycloak authentication manager in `apache-airflow-providers-keycloak` did not generate or validate the OAuth 2.0 `state` parameter on the login / login-callback flow, and did not use PKCE. An attacker with a Keycloak account in the same realm could deliver a crafted callback URL to a victim's browser and cause the victim to be logged into the attacker's Airflow session (login-CSRF / session fixation), where any credentials the victim subsequently stored in Airflow Connections would be harve

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40948.

title: The Categories Images plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting in...
description: The Categories Images plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting in versions up to, and including, 3.3.1, via the 'z_taxonomy_image' shortcode. This is due to the shortcode rendering path passing attacker-controlled class input into a fallback image builder that concatenates HTML attributes without proper escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts that execute when users interact wi

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with Contributor-level access or higher to inject malicious scripts, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or defacement of affected WordPress sites. This could compromise user trust and site integrity.
recommended_actions: Update the Categories Images plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable it until a fix is released.
Restrict Contributor-level access to trusted users only and audit existing user permissions.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts.

title: The Contextual Related Posts plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting...
description: The Contextual Related Posts plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'other_attributes' parameter in versions up to, and including, 4.2.1 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2986.

title: The Content Blocks (Custom Post Widget) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site...
description: The Content Blocks (Custom Post Widget) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the plugin's content_block shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 3.3.9 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied values consumed from user-created content blocks. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an inj

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with contributor-level access or higher to inject malicious scripts into content blocks, potentially leading to stored cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks. This could result in session hijacking, defacement, or unauthorized actions on affected websites.
recommended_actions: Update the Content Blocks (Custom Post Widget) plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict contributor-level access to trusted users and review their permissions to minimize the risk of exploitation.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts targeting this vulnerability.

title: Little CMS (lcms2) through 2.18 has an integer overflow in CubeSize in cmslut.c because the...
description: Little CMS (lcms2) through 2.18 has an integer overflow in CubeSize in cmslut.c because the overflow check is performed after the multiplication.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41254.

title: Secrets in Variables saved as JSON dictionaries were not properly redacted - in case thee...
description:
Secrets in Variables saved as JSON dictionaries were not properly redacted - in case thee variables were retrieved by the user the secrets stored as nested fields were not masked.

If you do not store variables with sensitive values in JSON form, you are not affected. Otherwise please upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.2.0 that has the fix implemented

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-32690.

title: In case of SQL errors, exception/stack trace of errors was exposed in API even if "api...
description: In case of SQL errors, exception/stack trace of errors was exposed in API even if "api/expose_stack_traces" was set to false. That could lead to exposing additional information to potential attacker. Users are recommended to upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.2.0, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The exposure of SQL exception and stack trace information in the API could allow attackers to gain insights into the database structure, query logic, or other sensitive implementation details. This information could be leveraged for further attacks, such as SQL injection or reconnaissance for system vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.2.0 or later to patch the vulnerability and prevent stack trace exposure.
Review and implement strict error-handling mechanisms to ensure sensitive information is not exposed in API responses.
Conduct a security audit of the API to identify and mitigate any additional vulnerabilities or misconfigurations.

title: An example of BashOperator in Airflow documentation suggested a way of passing dag_run.conf in...
description: An example of BashOperator in Airflow documentation suggested a way of passing dag_run.conf in the way that could cause unsanitized user input to be used to escalate privileges of UI user to allow execute code on worker. Users should review if any of their own DAGs have adopted this incorrect advice.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unsanitized user input to be executed, potentially escalating privileges and enabling remote code execution on Airflow workers. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Review all DAGs in your Airflow environment to identify and remove any instances where unsanitized user input from 'dag_run.conf' is passed to BashOperator.
Apply input sanitization and validation to ensure no untrusted or malicious input can be executed.
Monitor for updates or patches from Apache Airflow and apply them immediately to address CVE-2026-30898.

title:
UI / API User with asset materialize permission could trigger dags they had no access to.
Users...
description:
UI / API User with asset materialize permission could trigger dags they had no access to.
Users are advised to migrate to Airflow version 3.2.0 that fixes the issue.

title: Dag Authors, who normally should not be able to execute code in the webserver context could craft...
description:
Dag Authors, who normally should not be able to execute code in the webserver context could craft XCom payload causing the webserver to execute arbitrary code. Since Dag Authors are already highly trusted, severity of this issue is Low.

Users are recommended to upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.2.0, which fixes the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows Dag Authors to execute arbitrary code in the webserver context by crafting malicious XCom payloads. While the risk is mitigated by the fact that Dag Authors are already highly trusted users, exploitation could lead to unauthorized actions or compromise of the webserver.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.2.0 or the latest patched version to address the vulnerability.
Restrict and monitor access to Dag Author roles to ensure only trusted personnel have access.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect unusual activity related to XCom payloads.

title: The CMP – Coming Soon & Maintenance Plugin by NiteoThemes plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to...
description: The CMP – Coming Soon & Maintenance Plugin by NiteoThemes plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file upload and remote code execution in all versions up to, and including, 4.1.16 via the `cmp_theme_update_install` AJAX action. This is due to the function only checking for the `publish_pages` capability (available to Editors and above) instead of `manage_options` (Administrators only), combined with a lack of proper validation on the user-supplied file URL and no verification of the dow

This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a

title: The Flipbox Addon for Elementor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting...
description: The Flipbox Addon for Elementor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the Flipbox widget's button URL `custom_attributes` field in all versions up to, and including, 2.1.1 due to insufficient validation of custom attribute names. Specifically, the plugin uses `esc_html()` on the attribute name which does not prevent event handler attributes (e.g., `onmouseover`, `onclick`). This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with author-level access and above, to

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6048.

title: In iTerm2 through 3.6.9, displaying a .txt file can cause code execution via DCS 2000p and OSC...
description: In iTerm2 through 3.6.9, displaying a .txt file can cause code execution via DCS 2000p and OSC 135 data, if the working directory contains a malicious file whose name is valid output from the conductor encoding path, such as a pathname with an initial ace/c+ substring, aka "hypothetical in-band signaling abuse." This occurs because iTerm2 accepts the SSH conductor protocol from terminal output that does not originate from a legitimate conductor session.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in iTerm2 through 3.6.9 allows for potential remote code execution when displaying a malicious .txt file. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or system compromise, particularly in environments where iTerm2 is widely used for development or administrative tasks.
recommended_actions: Update iTerm2 to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-41253.
Avoid opening untrusted or unknown .txt files in iTerm2, especially in directories containing unverified files.
Implement monitoring and logging to detect unusual terminal activity or unauthorized code execution.

title: The Youzify plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The Youzify plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'checkin_place_id' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 1.3.6 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1559.

title: The Hostel plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The Hostel plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting via the 'shortcode_id' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 1.1.6 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that execute if they can successfully trick a user into performing an action such as clicking on a link.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary scripts in the context of a user's browser, potentially leading to session hijacking, phishing attacks, or unauthorized actions performed on behalf of the user.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using WordPress
recommended_actions: Update the Hostel plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available or disable the plugin temporarily if no update is provided.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests targeting the 'shortcode_id' parameter.
Educate users to avoid clicking on suspicious links, especially if they lead to pages using the vulnerable plugin.

title: Zebra Vulnerable to Consensus Divergence in Transparent Sighash Hash-Type Handling
description:
# Consensus Divergence in Transparent Sighash Hash-Type Handling

## Summary

After a refactoring, Zebra failed to validate a consensus rule that restricted the possible values of sighash hash types for V5 transactions which were enabled in the NU5 network upgrade. Zebra nodes could thus accept and eventually mine a block that would be considered invalid by zcashd nodes, creating a consensus split between Zebra and zcashd nodes.

In a similar vein, for V4 transactions, Zebra mistakenly used the

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity.

title: Zebra Vulnerable to Denial of Service via Interrupted JSON-RPC Requests from Authenticated Clients
description:
A vulnerability in Zebra's JSON-RPC HTTP middleware allows an authenticated RPC client to cause a Zebra node to crash by disconnecting before the request body is fully received. The node treats the failure to read the HTTP request body as an unrecoverable error and aborts the process instead of returning an error response.

## Severity
**Moderate** - This is a Denial of Service (DoS) that requires a client capable of passing Zebra's cookie authentication, which is enabled by default.

## Affecte

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an authenticated client to crash a Zebra node by exploiting a flaw in its JSON-RPC HTTP middleware. This could result in a denial of service, disrupting operations and availability of the affected systems.
recommended_actions: Apply any available patches or updates from Zebra to address the vulnerability as soon as possible.
Implement rate-limiting and monitoring for JSON-RPC requests to detect and mitigate potential abuse.
Restrict access to the JSON-RPC interface by using network-level controls, such as IP whitelisting or VPNs, to limit exposure to trusted clients only.

title: Zebra has rk Identity Point Panic in Transaction Verification
description:
# rk Identity Point Panic in Transaction Verification

## Summary

Orchard transactions contain a `rk` field which is a randomized validating key and also an elliptic curve point. The Zcash specification allows the field to be the identity (a "zero" value), however, the `orchard` crate which is used to verify Orchard proofs would panic when fed a `rk` with the identity value. Thus an attacker could send a crafted transaction that would make a Zebra node crash.

## Severity

**Critical** - This i

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity.

title: CVE-2026-6119 - A vulnerability was identified in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. The affected element is the func
description: A vulnerability was identified in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. The affected element is the function post_data.get of the component API Endpoint. Such manipulation leads to server-side request forgery. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit a server-side request forgery (SSRF) flaw in the API Endpoint, potentially enabling unauthorized access to internal systems, sensitive data exfiltration, or lateral movement within the network. The availability of a public exploit increases the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization to the 'post_data.get' function to prevent malicious payloads from being processed.
Implement network-level restrictions to limit access to internal systems from the affected API Endpoint.
Monitor for unusual API activity and apply patches or updates as soon as they are released by AstrBotDevs.

title:
CVE-2026-31413 - In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Fix unsound scalar forking
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Fix unsound scalar forking in maybe_fork_scalars() for BPF_OR

maybe_fork_scalars() is called for both BPF_AND and BPF_OR when the
source operand is a constant.  When dst has signed range [-1, 0], it
forks the verifier state: the pushed path gets dst = 0, the current
path gets dst = -1.

For BPF_AND this is correct: 0 & K == 0.
For BPF_OR this is wrong:    0 | K == K, not 0.

The pushed path therefore tracks dst as 0 when the runtime value is K,
producing an exploitable verifier/runtime divergence that allows
out-of-bounds map access.

Fix this by passing env->insn_idx (instead of env->insn_idx + 1) to
push_stack(), so the pushed path re-executes the ALU instruction with
dst = 0 and naturally computes the correct result for any opcode.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31413.

title: CVE-2026-6118 - A vulnerability was determined in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. Impacted is the function add_mcp
description: A vulnerability was determined in AstrBotDevs AstrBot up to 4.22.1. Impacted is the function add_mcp_server of the file astrbot/dashboard/routes/tools.py of the component MCP Endpoint. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

title: CVE-2026-1116 - A Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `from_dict` method of the `AppLollm
description: A Cross-site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `from_dict` method of the `AppLollmsMessage` class in parisneo/lollms prior to version 2.2.0. The vulnerability arises from the lack of sanitization or HTML encoding of the `content` field when deserializing user-provided data. This allows an attacker to inject malicious HTML or JavaScript payloads, which can be executed in the context of another user's browser. Exploitation of this vulnerability can lead to account takeover, session hijacking, or wormable attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1116.

This manipulation of the argument Name causes cross site scripting. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. Upgrading to version 2.8.0 is capable of addressing this issue. The vendor was contacted early, responded in a very professional manner and quickly released a fixed version of the affected product.
